title: The Young Canopy Station

features.name: unindexed-sapling
features.desc-on-touch: A young tree still learning its own name. The archive hasn't filed its location yet, hasn't categorized its growth-rate. You stand near something the system is still deciding whether to remember.

features.name: fence-post-row
features.desc-on-touch: A line of fence posts marking where the plantation ends, where the archived-orchard of planted things becomes the margin where new things grow without permission. The archive wants to catalogue this boundary. The boundary moves.

features.name: seedling-vault
features.desc-on-touch: Where seeds are kept before they're assigned to trees. The archive stores futures here—potential growth-paths, unborn broadcasts, the catalogue of what might happen if the canopy thinned.

features.name: fresh-leaf-archive
features.desc-on-touch: Leaves so new they haven't learned to read yet. The archive doesn't know what to do with growth that hasn't stopped growing. It keeps updating the file, never finishing.

features.name: canopy-edge-marker
features.desc-on-touch: The line between catalogued growth and wild growth. On this side, everything is indexed. On that side, trees are still surprising the system with what they do.

inscription: young leaves don't know they're being read, and that silence is what lets them grow

file.name: Canopy_Edge_Growth_Projection_1995-ONGOING.log
file.note: Predictions of which young trees will mature, which will fall, which will change frequency. Every prediction is marked UNFINISHED. The future is still growing.

exits.label: returning to where the understory is still learning to remember
up toward the canopy where trees have stopped growing and started keeping records
along the canopy-edge toward the dense plantation zones
back into the thick understory where even young things have learned to hide
